approaches.title: Multi-Token Prediction Auxiliary Loss (zero code changes)
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: Zero code changes required -- just set MTP_NUM_HEADS=1 MTP_LOSS_WEIGHT=0.15. The entire MTP infrastructure already exists and is tested (lines 866-962). MTP heads are correctly excluded from export (line 1778). Zero artifact impact. The highest ratio of expected gain to implementation effort on the entire list.

approaches.title: Sequence Curriculum: Short-to-Long Schedule
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: The ternary codebase already prototyped SEQ_LEN_START and SEQ_SCHEDULE_FRACTION env vars, proving the concept is implementable. For Stack A, this is a ~20-line change to the training loop. Expected ~3600 extra training steps in the first 30% from 512 seq_len (50ms/step vs 83ms). High confidence based on training efficiency literature.

approaches.title: Combo A: Depth Recurrence + MoD + Cross-Layer KV Sharing
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: Highest ceiling hypothesis in the entire bank (0.005-0.015 BPB). Three mutually reinforcing techniques that address the core bottleneck: model capacity within 16MB. MoD cancels the compute overhead of recurrence, and KV sharing is free with recurrence. High risk but the potential payoff is 3-5x larger than any single low-risk hypothesis. Ranked 3rd because the expected value (ceiling * probability) still exceeds most safe bets.

approaches.title: Late QAT Threshold Tuning + Dual-Phase QAT
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: Pure hyperparameter sweep -- just change LATE_QAT_THRESHOLD env var. The dual-phase variant (int8 at scale<0.3, int6 at scale<0.15) is a 5-line code change to CastedLinear.forward. The ternary entry trains with quantization from step 0, suggesting earlier QAT is viable. Low risk, low effort.

approaches.title: Mixture of Depth (MoD): Per-Layer Token Routing
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: Novel DarkLab idea with strong theoretical backing (Raposo et al. 2024). Zero parameter cost, 15-25% step time reduction yielding 1000-1500 extra training steps. Unlike MoE (H16), MoD does not increase artifact size. Can be tested independently before committing to the full Combo A (H36). Medium risk because router training stability needs validation.

approaches.title: TTT Optimizer Upgrade: Adam + Per-Layer LR
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: Direct improvement to the existing TTT code path (lines 1110-1229). The current SGD optimizer (line 1128) is the simplest possible choice. Adam with per-layer LR is a ~10-line change that leverages the existing param grouping by frozen/unfrozen blocks. TTT already contributes 0.0024 BPB (1.1218 -> 1.1194); a better optimizer could push this to 0.003-0.005.

approaches.title: Combo B: Spectral Reparam + Progressive QAT + Per-Row Adaptive Bitwidth
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: Quantization is the most predictable source of BPB loss (the gap between fp16 and int6 weights is measurable). This combo attacks quantization at all three phases: training (spectral), transition (progressive QAT), and export (per-row bitwidth). Each component is individually low-medium risk; combined they represent a systematic quantization improvement strategy. Lower rank than Combo A because the ceiling is smaller (0.003-0.008 vs 0.005-0.015).

approaches.title: EMA Decay Schedule (Warm-Start EMA)
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: One-line change to ema_decay computation (line 1637 area). Low risk, zero artifact impact. The current fixed 0.997 is arbitrary; scheduling to match lr_mul dynamics is principled. Expected 0.0005-0.002 BPB for near-zero implementation effort.

approaches.title: SWA + EMA Ensemble Blending
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: The code already maintains both SWA and EMA states simultaneously. Currently only one is used. Blending is a post-training operation requiring zero retraining -- just evaluate 4 blend ratios on validation. Alpha sweep adds ~30s to export pipeline. Risk is essentially zero since we can fall back to pure EMA.

approaches.title: TurboQuant-Inspired Hadamard Rotation Before Weight Quantization
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: DOWNGRADED after EXP-H38-A/B: +0.03% at int8, +0.17% at int6 on raw weights — negligible. Only actionable finding: +17.3% MSE on QAT-shaped weights downquantized to int5, which is relevant only as a sub-technique inside H37 (mixed-precision per-row export). Moved to contingent on H37.

approaches.title: Full Depth Recurrence: 4 Unique Layers x 3x = 12 Virtual Layers
approaches.rationale_for_ranking: The DarkLab's most radical architectural proposal. At d_model=384 with 4 unique layers, the model fits in ~3MB at int6, leaving massive headroom for wider MLP or larger vocab. 12 virtual layers exceed the current 11 physical layers. Ranked 10th (not higher) because it requires a full architecture rewrite and the risk of recurrence instability is substantial. However, if validated, this becomes the foundation for Combo A (H36).
